illinois.h2: What Illinois families should know about the social media lawsuits
illinois.body:
Cases filed by Illinois residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Illinois family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Illinois’s general personal injury deadline is 2 years (735 ILCS 5/13-202). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Illinois treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Illinois courts apply a discovery rule in many injury cases, so the clock may run from when the injury and its likely cause were or should have been discovered. Product liability actions are also subject to statutory repose periods (735 ILCS 5/13-213). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Illinois claim.

sources.label: U.S. District Court, N.D. Cal. — MDL-3047 case page
JPML — pending MDL dockets

faqs.question: Where are Illinois social media addiction cases handled?
faqs.answer: Federal cases filed by Illinois residents are transferred into MDL-3047 in the Northern District of California, and many individual cases also proceed in California's JCCP. Illinois's personal injury deadline is 2 years (735 ILCS 5/13-202), and a minor's deadline is commonly paused — a lawyer can confirm the timeline.

missouri.h2: What Missouri families should know about the social media lawsuits
missouri.body:
Cases filed by Missouri residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Missouri family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Missouri’s general personal injury deadline is 5 years (Mo. Rev. Stat. § 516.120(4)). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Missouri treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Missouri's period generally runs from when damage is sustained and capable of ascertainment, which can function like a discovery rule in latent-injury cases. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Missouri claim.

texas.h2: What Texas families should know about the social media lawsuits
texas.body:
Cases filed by Texas residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Texas family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Texas’s general personal injury deadline is 2 years (Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 16.003). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Texas treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Texas recognizes a discovery rule in limited circumstances involving inherently undiscoverable injuries. Texas applies a 15-year statute of repose to many product liability claims (§ 16.012). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Texas claim.

florida.h2: What Florida families should know about the social media lawsuits
florida.body:
Cases filed by Florida residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Florida family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Florida’s general personal injury deadline is 4 years (Fla. Stat. § 95.11(3)(d)). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Florida treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Florida applies a four-year period to injury actions founded on the design, manufacture, distribution, or sale of personal property. Florida's 2023 tort reform (HB 837) cut the general negligence period to two years (§ 95.11(4)(a)) but did not change the product-liability period at § 95.11(3)(d). Latent-injury accrual rules can apply. Florida product liability claims can be subject to a 12-year repose period for certain products (§ 95.031). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Florida claim.

california.h2: Why California is central to the social media lawsuits
california.body:
California is the center of this litigation. The federal MDL-3047 is based in the Northern District of California before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers, and the parallel state-court coordination — the “Social Media Cases,” JCCP 5255 — proceeds before Judge Carolyn Kuhl in Los Angeles Superior Court. The first JCCP personal-injury bellwether returned a $6 million verdict against Meta and YouTube in March 2026 (Snap and TikTok had settled that case before trial), and a second personal-injury bellwether is set for July 27, 2026.

California’s general personal injury deadline is two years (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. § 335.1), and the clock is commonly paused while a claimant is a minor. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific California claim.

sources.label: U.S. District Court, N.D. Cal. — MDL-3047 case page
Law.com (The Recorder) — first JCCP bellwether verdict and YouTube settlement (June 2026)

faqs.question: Why is California central to the social media lawsuits?
faqs.answer: Both the federal MDL-3047 and the California JCCP (Social Media Cases, JCCP 5255) are based in California, and the first JCCP bellwether produced a $6 million verdict against Meta and YouTube in March 2026. California's personal injury deadline is two years (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 335.1), commonly paused while a claimant is a minor.

north-carolina.h2: What North Carolina families should know about the social media lawsuits
north-carolina.body:
Cases filed by North Carolina residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a North Carolina family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

North Carolina’s general personal injury deadline is 3 years (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 1-52). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how North Carolina treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. North Carolina applies discovery-based accrual to many latent injury claims (§ 1-52(16)). North Carolina applies a 12-year statute of repose to product liability actions (§ 1-46.1). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific North Carolina claim.

georgia.h2: What Georgia families should know about the social media lawsuits
georgia.body:
Cases filed by Georgia residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Georgia family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Georgia’s general personal injury deadline is 2 years (O.C.G.A. § 9-3-33). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Georgia treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Georgia courts have applied discovery-based accrual in certain latent-injury and toxic exposure cases. Georgia applies a 10-year statute of repose to many product liability claims (O.C.G.A. § 51-1-11(b)(2)). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Georgia claim.

ohio.h2: What Ohio families should know about the social media lawsuits
ohio.body:
Cases filed by Ohio residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Ohio family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Ohio’s general personal injury deadline is 2 years (Ohio Rev. Code § 2305.10). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Ohio treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Ohio's statute includes discovery-based accrual provisions for certain exposure-related claims. Ohio applies a 10-year statute of repose to many product liability claims (§ 2305.10(C)). A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Ohio claim.

pennsylvania.h2: What Pennsylvania families should know about the social media lawsuits
pennsylvania.body:
Cases filed by Pennsylvania residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Pennsylvania family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Pennsylvania’s general personal injury deadline is 2 years (42 Pa. Cons. Stat. § 5524). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Pennsylvania treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Pennsylvania applies a discovery rule that can delay accrual until the injury and its cause reasonably could have been discovered. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Pennsylvania claim.

Cases filed by Michigan residents in federal court are transferred into MDL-3047 before Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers in the Northern District of California, where 2,664 cases were pending as of June 1, 2026; many individual cases also proceed in California’s JCCP 5255. Where a Michigan family lives does not change the addictive-design and failure-to-warn theories at the center of the cases.

Michigan’s general personal injury deadline is 3 years (Mich. Comp. Laws § 600.5805(2)). Because these claims usually involve harm that began when the user was a child or teenager, how Michigan treats a minor’s filing deadline is often decisive — many states pause the clock until a young person reaches adulthood. Michigan applies limited discovery-based accrual rules; latent-injury treatment varies by claim type. A lawyer can confirm how the deadline applies to a specific Michigan claim.
